description_long: This handbag features a woven, basket-like exterior in natural tan and brown hues, accented by vertical black stripes that add a touch of geometric contrast. The upper rim and handles are constructed from black leather or faux leather, with gold-tone hardware securing the handles and strap attachments. The bag has a structured, slightly tapered silhouette with a wide opening at the top, closed by a prominent black flap and likely a magnetic snap or similar mechanism. Two types of straps are present: a pair of short, sturdy handles for hand carrying and a longer, adjustable crossbody or shoulder strap attached with gold hardware. The woven body suggests a lightweight yet durable construction, while the black leather elements provide both reinforcement and a modern urban aesthetic. The overall design is both functional and stylish, suitable for everyday use in city environments.
description: A woven tan and brown handbag with black leather handles, gold hardware, and a black flap closure, featuring both short and long adjustable straps. The design is structured, stylish, and ideal for urban use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the woven texture, black leather handle, and the attachment of the long adjustable strap.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the black flap closure, gold hardware, and the symmetrical woven pattern with vertical black stripes.
description_view_2: Opposite side view similar to view 0, displaying the woven body and strap attachment details.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the black flap, handle attachments, and the continuation of the woven pattern.
attribute_strap_type: Two short black leather handles and one long adjustable black crossbody/shoulder strap, attached with gold hardware.
attribute_closure_mechanism: Black flap closure at the top center, likely secured with a magnetic snap or button.
attribute_decorative_elements: Gold-tone hardware at handle and strap attachments.
attribute_pattern: Woven basket pattern with vertical black stripes.
